name: Grilled Shark with Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse shark with cold water; pat dry with paper towels.
2. Set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, melt butter over low heat.
4. Add onion and garlic to the saucepan; sauté until tender but not browned.
5. Stir in the remaining ingredients (water, vinegar, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, catsup).
6. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring frequently.
7. Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce is thickened.
8. Remove from heat.
9. Baste the shark with the sauce.
10. Place the shark on a well-greased grate, about 4-5 inches from hot coals.
11. Cook for 4-5 minutes on each side.
12. Baste and turn the shark; cook an additional 4-5 minutes, or until it flakes easily with a fork.
13. Makes 6 servings.
NOTE: Shark may be broiled on a well-greased broiler pan, 5-6 inches from the source of heat. Follow grilling times and directions.
